senses.definition: something or someone that is familiar, comfortable, and easy to be around

examples.x: Despite not seeing each other for years, when they met again, it was like slipping into an old shoe, comfortable and easy.
The veteran employee was considered an old shoe in the company, as he knew the ins and outs of the business.
Even in a new city, she quickly made friends and established an old shoe relationship with her neighbors.
The teacher had been in the profession for decades and had a reputation for being an old shoe, always reliable and supportive.

senses.definition: the forefront or leading position in a particular field or area of activity, characterized by the most advanced or innovative practices, technologies, or ideas
(of a piece of equipment) very advanced in design
something that is considered the most fashionable or advanced in comparison with others of the same type

examples.x: The new luxury car model is the last word in automotive engineering, incorporating the latest advancements in performance and safety.
The fashion designer's collection is always the final word in style, setting trends that influence the entire industry.
The smartphone's sleek design, powerful features, and intuitive user interface make it the last word in mobile technology.
The acclaimed scientist's groundbreaking research findings are regarded as the final word in the field of astrophysics.
The critically acclaimed restaurant's tasting menu is the last word in culinary innovation, showcasing a unique blend of flavors and techniques.

word_list.word: the shape of things to come
word_list.pronunciation: ðə ʃˈeɪp ʌv θˈɪŋz tə kˈʌm

senses.definition: a short glance of what one is likely to experience or what might happen in the future

examples.x: The new technological breakthrough offers a glimpse into the shape of things to come, hinting at the possibilities of future advancements.
The young prodigy's exceptional talent in music is a clear indication of the shape of things to come, foreshadowing a promising career.
The company's successful expansion in international markets is a sign of the shape of things to come, pointing towards global dominance.

senses.definition: someone or something that presents a refreshing change compared to what existed before

examples.x: The new manager's leadership style was a breath of fresh air, fostering open communication and a collaborative work environment.
After years of monotonous routine, the vacation was a breath of fresh air, rejuvenating the weary travelers.
The young artist's bold and innovative approach to painting brought a breath of fresh air to the traditional art scene.
The new restaurant in town offers a unique culinary experience, providing a breath of fresh air among the usual dining options.
The introduction of flexible work hours was a breath of fresh air for the employees, allowing them to achieve a better work-life balance.

senses.definition: to age gradually, leading to a state of poor or declining condition

examples.x: The abandoned house had fallen to pieces, with crumbling walls and a sagging roof.
After years of neglect, the once-thriving garden had fallen to pieces, overrun by weeds and dead plants.
The old book, left exposed to moisture and pests, had fallen to pieces, its pages yellowed and torn.
The neglected car had fallen to pieces, with rust eating away at its body and engine failure.
The antique furniture, without proper care, had fallen to pieces, with loose joints and chipped surfaces.

senses.definition: (of objects) in a perfect or unharmed condition

examples.x: The vintage comic book was in mint condition, with no creases, tears, or signs of wear.
The collector's car, meticulously stored and maintained, was in mint condition despite its age.
The antique watch, kept in a display case and rarely worn, remained in mint condition with its original parts intact.
The limited-edition vinyl record, never played and stored in a protective sleeve, was in mint condition, retaining its high audio quality.
The sports memorabilia, signed by the athlete and stored in a sealed case, was in mint condition, preserving its value and authenticity.

senses.definition: used to describe something that has been used many times or has existed for an extended period of time and is no longer new

examples.x: Wearing a tuxedo to the office these days is old hat; casual attire is the new norm.
His jokes about smartphones being cutting-edge technology are old hat; we've heard them a million times.
For the fashion-forward, last year's trends are already old hat; they're always looking for the latest styles.
In the fast-paced tech industry, what's new today can become old hat in a matter of months.
She thought her idea for a smartphone app was groundbreaking, but by the time she developed it, it was already old hat.

senses.definition: to be ignored or left unused for a very long time

examples.x: The old vinyl records in the attic have been gathering dust for decades, untouched and forgotten.
His artistic talents, once vibrant and celebrated, now collect dust as he pursues a corporate career.
The unfinished novel lay on the shelf, gathering dust, as the author struggled with writer's block.
The outdated computer equipment was left to collect dust in the storage room, replaced by newer models.
The abandoned project had great potential but ultimately collected dust as other priorities took precedence.

senses.definition: a business that has both real physical stores and also sells products or services online through a website or app

examples.x: The successful retailer embraced a bricks and clicks strategy, offering customers the convenience of online shopping while maintaining a physical store presence.
The company's expansion into e-commerce was a strategic move towards embracing the bricks and clicks model, catering to the preferences of their diverse customer base.
The bricks and clicks approach allowed the bookstore to thrive in the digital age by combining an online platform with their physical store, offering customers a seamless shopping experience.
The integration of online ordering and in-store pickup was a key initiative in the company's bricks and clicks strategy, providing customers with flexibility and convenience.

The department store's adoption of a bricks and clicks model resulted in increased sales and customer satisfaction, as shoppers could browse products online and visit the store for a hands-on experience before making a purchase.

senses.definition: physical stores or buildings in contrast to online or virtual alternatives

examples.x: The company decided to invest in bricks and mortar by opening a new flagship store in the city center.
Despite the rise of e-commerce, many customers still prefer the experience of shopping in bricks and mortar stores.
The real estate market remains a reliable investment option, as people continue to invest in bricks and mortar properties.
The bookstore faced tough competition from online retailers but managed to thrive due to its loyal customer base and bricks and mortar presence.
The clothing brand expanded from online-only operations to establish bricks and mortar stores, allowing customers to try on and experience the products in person.

senses.definition: (of a paper, particularly a newspaper) recently printed

examples.x: The breaking news article was hot off the press, providing readers with the most recent updates on the situation.
Grab a copy of the newspaper while it's still hot off the press to stay informed about the latest events.
The magazine featured an exclusive interview with the celebrity, offering readers insights hot off the press.
The morning edition of the newspaper, hot off the press, was delivered to doorsteps across the city, ensuring readers were up to date with the latest news.

senses.definition: to become outdated and no longer liked or supported

examples.x: Bell-bottom pants fell out of fashion in the 1980s but have recently made a comeback.
That hairstyle went out of fashion years ago; you should consider trying something more current.
The neon colors that were popular last season have already gone out of style.
Traditional wedding dresses are timeless and never go out of fashion, unlike trendy alternatives.
The bulky cellphones from the early 2000s have completely gone out of fashion with the rise of slim and sleek smartphones.
In the 90s, wearing oversized clothing was the trend, but now it's completely fallen out of style.
Flip phones have fallen out of fashion with the rise of smartphones.
Neon colors fell out of fashion for a while but have recently become trendy again.
In academic circles, some theories have fallen out of style as new evidence emerges.
His conservative political views have fallen out of fashion with the younger generation.
The use of formal language and expressions has fallen out of style in modern literature, where more casual and colloquial language is preferred.
The minimalist design trend caused elaborate Victorian furniture to fall out of fashion.
With the rise of ergonomic office chairs, traditional wooden desks have gone out of style in many workplaces.
